goal: Land the two external-binary plugin manifests for Phase 2 Week 6: `plugins/structural/ast-grep/plugin.toml` (P2-W6-F05) and `plugins/search/probe/plugin.toml` (P2-W6-F06). Each manifest's `transport.command` MUST launch a real stdio MCP server for its respective tool — no mocks. Each manifest is parsed by the existing `PluginManifest::from_path` (no schema changes), validated end-to-end via a real `PluginManager::health_check` round-trip in a new `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` integration suite, and gated by two acceptance scripts at `scripts/verify/P2-W6-F05.sh` and `scripts/verify/P2-W6-F06.sh` that perform an end-to-end probe against the fixture projects. No source-code changes to the daemon plugin parser or lifecycle code — those landed in WO-0042 / WO-0043 and stay frozen.
plan_summary: Three groups of artefacts with zero overlap: (A) Two new plugin manifest TOMLs at `plugins/structural/ast-grep/plugin.toml` and `plugins/search/probe/plugin.toml`. Both follow the existing `plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml` shape: `[plugin]` (name, version, description), `[capabilities]` (provides + languages), `[transport]` (stdio with a pinned canonical MCP-server launch command — e.g. `npx -y @ast-grep/...@<pinned-version>` or `uvx --from <pinned-git-ref> <bin>` or `cargo run --manifest-path ... --release`), `[lifecycle]` (hot_cold = true; idle_timeout_minutes = 10). Versions MUST be pinned to a specific tag/commit per `plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml:11-14` reproducibility precedent. (B) One new integration test file `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` with two `#[tokio::test]` async tests — `ast_grep_manifest_health_check` and `probe_manifest_health_check` — each: loads the on-disk manifest via `PluginManifest::from_path("plugins/<group>/<name>/plugin.toml")`, calls `PluginManager::health_check(&manifest)` which spawns a REAL subprocess and exchanges a real JSON-RPC `tools/list`, asserts `health.status == HealthStatus::Ok` and `health.tools` contains at least one expected tool-name string identified by the executor during research (e.g. ast-grep's `find_code` or `dump_syntax_tree`; probe's `search_code` or `query`). Tests are gated by `if std::env::var("UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS").is_ok() { return; }` ONLY for offline-CI but otherwise run unconditionally — the verifier MUST NOT set the skip env. (C) Two acceptance scripts `scripts/verify/P2-W6-F05.sh` and `scripts/verify/P2-W6-F06.sh` that: (1) print the binary version (`ast-grep --version` / `probe --version`) and exit 1 with a clear operator error if the binary is absent, (2) run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests <test_name>` and fail if non-zero, (3) run a focused end-to-end command exercising the fixture (F05: `ast-grep run -p '<pattern>' tests/fixtures/typescript-project` and grep for a known symbol; F06: `probe extract <known-fn> tests/fixtures/rust-project` and grep for the function body). (D) Two devtools install scripts `scripts/devtools/install-ast-grep.sh` and `scripts/devtools/install-probe.sh` that document and reproduce the binary install (no auto-execute side effects). NO changes to the existing daemon source code, NO new daemon Cargo deps, NO touching `plugins/structural/serena/`.
scope_in: plugins/structural/ast-grep/plugin.toml — NEW. Same structural shape as `plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml`. `[plugin]` table: `name = "ast-grep"`, `version = "<pinned upstream version e.g. 0.42.1>"`, `description = "Structural code search via tree-sitter AST patterns."`. `[capabilities]` table: `provides = ["search.structural", "rewrite.structural"]`, `languages = [...]` (executor picks the canonical 8-12 ast-grep-supported languages — at minimum rust, typescript, javascript, python, go, java). `[transport]` table: `type = "stdio"`, `command` and `args` set so the launched subprocess is a REAL MCP server speaking JSON-RPC over stdio (executor researches: ast-grep upstream may ship `ast-grep mcp` in 0.43+, or use `npx -y @ast-grep/<package>@<pinned-version>`, or another pinned canonical invocation — pick ONE and document the choice in a leading TOML comment block citing the upstream URL + commit/tag — same pattern as serena's `plugin.toml:1-14`). `[lifecycle]`: `hot_cold = true`, `idle_timeout_minutes = 10`. NO `main`-branch refs; pin to a tag or commit SHA.

plugins/search/probe/plugin.toml — NEW. `[plugin]`: `name = "probe"`, `version = "<pinned upstream version>"`, `description = "AST-aware code search with token-budgeted function-body extraction (buger/probe)."`. `[capabilities]`: `provides = ["search.semantic", "search.text"]`, `languages = [...]` (probe-supported set; minimum rust, typescript, javascript, python, go). `[transport]`: same pinned-canonical-MCP-launch principle as ast-grep. Probe upstream is https://github.com/buger/probe — executor researches the canonical MCP-server invocation (likely `npx -y @buger/probe-mcp@<pinned>` or a `probe mcp` subcommand or `uvx --from git+https://github.com/buger/probe@<tag> probe-mcp` — pick ONE pinned variant and cite the upstream URL + version in a leading comment). `[lifecycle]`: `hot_cold = true`, `idle_timeout_minutes = 10`.

scripts/devtools/install-ast-grep.sh — NEW. Idempotent shell script: detect existing `ast-grep` on PATH (`command -v ast-grep`), print version + exit 0 if present and version matches the manifest pin. Otherwise print the recommended install command (e.g. `cargo install ast-grep --version <pinned>` or `npm install -g @ast-grep/cli@<pinned>`) and exit 0 (do NOT auto-run the install — operators may choose how). Mark as executable (`chmod +x`).
scripts/devtools/install-probe.sh — NEW. Same idempotent pattern: detect, print version, otherwise print the recommended install command. NO side-effects.

crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs — NEW integration test file. Wrap tests in a `mod plugin_manifests { ... }` block so nextest reports them under `plugin_manifests::ast_grep_manifest_health_check` etc. — same pattern as the existing `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manager.rs:21` `mod plugin_manager { }` wrapper. Two `#[tokio::test]` async tests: `ast_grep_manifest_health_check` and `probe_manifest_health_check`. Each: (1) loads the manifest via `PluginManifest::from_path(workspace_root.join("plugins/<group>/<name>/plugin.toml"))` — discover the workspace root via `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")` + `..` traversal to repo root. (2) Calls `PluginManager::health_check(&manifest).await.expect("health-check")`. (3) Asserts `health.name == "<name>"`, `health.status == HealthStatus::Ok`, `!health.tools.is_empty()`, and `health.tools.iter().any(|t| t == "<expected-tool-name>")` — executor pins the expected tool name based on what the upstream MCP server actually advertises (run health_check once during dev to discover, then bake the assertion). NO mocks of `tokio::process::Command`. NO `#[ignore]`. The test file's docstring (per the plugin_manager.rs:21 pattern) explicitly cites the no-mock invariant + which workspace fixture projects the smoke command exercises.

scripts/verify/P2-W6-F05.sh — REWRITE the stub (current stub is `echo TODO; exit 1`). The script (a) sets `set -euo pipefail`, (b) `cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"`, (c) checks `command -v ast-grep` and exits 1 with operator-actionable message + `scripts/devtools/install-ast-grep.sh` reference if absent, (d) prints `ast-grep --version` for the verifier log, (e) runs `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests plugin_manifests::ast_grep_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | tee /tmp/wo-0044-f05-cargo.log` and `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed' /tmp/wo-0044-f05-cargo.log` (alternation pattern matching both cargo-test and cargo-nextest, lesson from WO-0038/0039/0042/0043), (f) runs a structural-search smoke test against `tests/fixtures/typescript-project` — e.g. `ast-grep run --pattern '<pattern>' --lang ts tests/fixtures/typescript-project | tee /tmp/wo-0044-f05-search.log` and `grep -q '<known-symbol-name>' /tmp/wo-0044-f05-search.log` to prove the search returned a result. The pattern + symbol-name pair is chosen by the executor based on what's actually in the fixture (read tests/fixtures/typescript-project/src/ to find a real symbol; do NOT modify the fixture — `tests/fixtures/**` is in forbidden_paths). (g) On all-green prints `[OK] P2-W6-F05` and exits 0; on any failure prints `[FAIL] P2-W6-F05: <reason>` and exits 1. `chmod +x` so it's executable.

scripts/verify/P2-W6-F06.sh — REWRITE the stub. Same shape as P2-W6-F05.sh but for probe + `tests/fixtures/rust-project`: (a-d) preamble, version print, install hint, (e) `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests plugin_manifests::probe_manifest_health_check`, (f) function-body extraction smoke against the rust-project fixture — `probe extract '<known-fn-name>' tests/fixtures/rust-project | tee /tmp/wo-0044-f06-extract.log` and `grep -q 'fn <known-fn-name>' /tmp/wo-0044-f06-extract.log` to assert the function body was returned. Token-budgeting: include a `--max-tokens=<small-N>` flag in the smoke command (per the F06 description's `with token budgets` clause); assert the output is bounded (`wc -c < /tmp/wo-0044-f06-extract.log` returns a value below a sane upper bound, e.g. 16384 chars). Same OK/FAIL exit semantics as F05.

Commit ladder — approximately 6 commits, each ≤50 LOC except the integration-test file (DEC-0005 module-coherence-commits applies): (1) `feat(plugins): add ast-grep plugin manifest under plugins/structural/ast-grep/` (~30 LOC TOML + comment block), (2) `feat(plugins): add probe plugin manifest under plugins/search/probe/` (~30 LOC), (3) `chore(devtools): add install-ast-grep.sh + install-probe.sh helpers` (~40 LOC across two files), (4) `test(daemon): add plugin_manifests integration suite covering ast-grep + probe health-check` (~120 LOC; over-budget per DEC-0005 module-coherence precedent for a single coherent test file), (5) `feat(verify): implement scripts/verify/P2-W6-F05.sh end-to-end` (~50 LOC bash), (6) `feat(verify): implement scripts/verify/P2-W6-F06.sh end-to-end` (~50 LOC bash).
scope_out: NO changes to ANY Rust source under `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**`. The plugin manifest parser (WO-0042) and lifecycle/hot-reload/circuit-breaker code (WO-0042/0043) is FROZEN for this WO. Both new manifests exercise existing `PluginManifest`, `PluginManager::health_check`, `PluginManager::discover` surfaces unchanged.

NO changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml`. The integration test imports `ucil_daemon::{PluginManifest, PluginManager, HealthStatus}` (already public via `lib.rs:105-109` post-WO-0042/0043) and uses `tempfile`, `tokio` (already in `[dev-dependencies]`). If a new dev-dep is genuinely required, escalate via ADR before adding.

NO changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manager.rs` (existing P2-W6-F01 integration suite — frozen regression guard). NO changes to `tests/e2e_mcp_stdio.rs`, `tests/e2e_mcp_with_kg.rs`, `tests/support/**`.
NO new daemon binary in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/bin/**`. Mock-MCP-plugin (P1-W3-F0X) stays untouched.
NO changes to `plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml` or any other existing plugin manifest. The serena manifest is frozen per WO-0013 acceptance.

NO new plugin under `plugins/architecture/`, `plugins/context/`, `plugins/knowledge/`, `plugins/platform/`, `plugins/quality/`, `plugins/testing/`, or `plugins/structural/scip/` / `joern/` etc. — those are out-of-phase or out-of-WO scope.

NO `ucil plugin` CLI subcommand (`list`, `install`, `uninstall`, `enable`, `disable`, `reload`) — that is feature P2-W6-F07 in a sibling WO. This WO MAY load manifests from on-disk paths but MUST NOT write a CLI command surface.
NO plugin lifecycle integration suite (`tests/integration/test_plugin_lifecycle.rs`) — that is feature P2-W6-F08 in a sibling WO and depends on F05/F06 (this WO).
NO G1 fusion logic, NO G2 RRF, NO `find_references` / `search_code` MCP tool wiring — those are P2-W7-F01..F09 features.
NO embedding pipeline / ONNX / LanceDB / CodeRankEmbed work — those are P2-W8-F01..F08 features.

NO modification to `tests/fixtures/typescript-project/**` or `tests/fixtures/rust-project/**`. Verify scripts must adapt to whatever symbols already exist in the fixtures — read the fixture source to find a real function/class name, do NOT add new ones.
NO `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, commented-out assertions, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.

NO mock of `tokio::process::Command`, `Child`, `ChildStdio`, or any other process-spawning surface in the new integration tests. The whole point of F05/F06 is that real MCP-server subprocesses speak real JSON-RPC over stdio.
NO `git commit --no-verify`, `--amend` after push, or force-push — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.
acceptance: `plugins/structural/ast-grep/plugin.toml` exists, parses cleanly via `PluginManifest::from_path`, has pinned `version` (no moving `main` ref), and lists `[capabilities]` + `[lifecycle]` sections (P2-W6-F05 manifest deliverable).
`plugins/search/probe/plugin.toml` exists, parses cleanly, has pinned `version`, and lists `[capabilities]` + `[lifecycle]` sections (P2-W6-F06 manifest deliverable).

`scripts/verify/P2-W6-F05.sh` exits 0 on a system with `ast-grep` on PATH; spawns the manifest's transport.command as a real subprocess; exchanges a real `tools/list` JSON-RPC; asserts at least one tool name; runs a structural pattern search against `tests/fixtures/typescript-project` and asserts a known symbol appears in the result.

`scripts/verify/P2-W6-F06.sh` exits 0 on a system with `probe` on PATH; spawns the probe MCP server; runs `tools/list`; runs a token-budgeted function-body extraction against `tests/fixtures/rust-project` and asserts a known function appears in the result.

`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests plugin_manifests::ast_grep_manifest_health_check` passes — REAL subprocess (no mocks), `health.status == HealthStatus::Ok`, `health.tools` non-empty.
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests plugin_manifests::probe_manifest_health_check` passes — REAL subprocess (no mocks), `health.status == HealthStatus::Ok`, `health.tools` non-empty.
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manager` still passes (P2-W6-F01 / WO-0009 regression guard — old minimal manifests still parse, integration suite still green).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_manifest_parser` still passes (P2-W6-F01 regression guard — manifest schema with capabilities + resources still parses).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_lifecycle_state_machine` still passes (P2-W6-F02 regression guard).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_hot_reload` still passes (P2-W6-F03 regression guard from WO-0043).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_circuit_breaker` still passes (P2-W6-F04 regression guard from WO-0043).

`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_with_kg` still passes (Phase-1 KG-bootstrap regression guard).
`cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` passes — no regression in any other crate.
`cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean (test file lint-clean, including `clippy::pedantic` defaults).
`cargo fmt --check` clean.

`shellcheck scripts/verify/P2-W6-F05.sh scripts/verify/P2-W6-F06.sh scripts/devtools/install-ast-grep.sh scripts/devtools/install-probe.sh` clean (or absent — if shellcheck is not on PATH, skip with a clear log line).

Mutation check (verifier runs): in `plugins/structural/ast-grep/plugin.toml`, replace the `[transport].command` field with `"/__ucil_test_nonexistent_astgrep_binary__"` → `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests plugin_manifests::ast_grep_manifest_health_check` fails with `PluginError::Spawn`. Restore → green. Proves the test exercises the real spawn path.

Restore → green.

rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (`thiserror` in libs; no `.unwrap()` outside `#[cfg(test)]` — N/A here since this WO has no daemon source changes; `tokio::time::timeout` on every IO await — health_check already wraps; integration tests against real processes mandatory)

rules:.claude/rules/commit-style.md (~50 LOC soft target; Conventional Commits with `Phase: 2` / `Feature: P2-W6-F05` or `P2-W6-F06` trailers; one feature per commit; no `--amend` after push; reproducibility — pinned versions only, no moving `main` refs)

phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/02-phase-2/CLAUDE.md (Phase 2 standing rules — no mocks of plugin runtimes; backward-compat for prior manifests; Phase 1 acceptance must stay green; lessons from WO-0042 + WO-0043 — pre-baked mutation checks, single-file blast radius, regression guards on every prior WO selector, cargo-test summary regex with alternation)
decision:DEC-0005-WO-0006-module-coherence-commits.md (test-introduction commits MAY exceed ~50 LOC — applies to commit 4, the integration test file)

decision:DEC-0007-remove-cargo-mutants-per-wo-gate.md (frozen-selector test placement — `mod plugin_manifests { }` wrapper for nextest path matching, matching the existing `mod plugin_manager { }` pattern in `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manager.rs:21`)

source:plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml (existing canonical manifest shape — pinned tag, leading reproducibility comment block, [plugin]/[transport]/[lifecycle] sections; ast-grep + probe manifests follow the same template)

source:crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs:104-122 (`PluginManifest` struct with `[capabilities]` and `[resources]` `#[serde(default)]` from WO-0042 — both new manifests can include or omit these tables)
source:crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs:264 (`PluginManifest::from_path` — entry point used by both new tests)
source:crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs:879-891 (`PluginManager::discover` walks a plugins dir, calls `from_path` per file — used as the manifest-loading reference)

source:crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs:946-1042 (`PluginManager::health_check` — real spawn + JSON-RPC `tools/list`; return type `PluginHealth` with `status: HealthStatus`, `tools: Vec<String>`, `name: String`)

source:crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manager.rs:21-128 (`mod plugin_manager { }` integration test pattern — `#[tokio::test]` async tests, real subprocess via `mock_plugin_path()`, no mocks; ast-grep / probe tests use the SAME shape but point at the real on-disk manifests rather than the mock)

dependency_readiness_proof.P2-W6-F01: passes=true (verifier-signed via WO-0042). Provides `PluginManifest::from_path`, the `[plugin]` / `[capabilities]` / `[transport]` / `[resources]` / `[lifecycle]` schema, and `PluginManager::discover` — all consumed (read-only) by this WO's integration tests.

lessons_applied: Frozen-selector placement (DEC-0007) — wrap the new integration tests in `mod plugin_manifests { }` (matching the existing `mod plugin_manager { }` block in `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manager.rs:21`) so nextest reports them under `plugin_manifests::ast_grep_manifest_health_check` / `plugin_manifests::probe_manifest_health_check`.

Backward-compat regression guards (WO-0042 / WO-0043 carry-forward) — `acceptance_criteria` runs every prior WO's frozen selector explicitly (`test_manifest_parser`, `test_lifecycle_state_machine`, `test_hot_reload`, `test_circuit_breaker`, `test_hot_cold_lifecycle`, `--test plugin_manager`, `--test e2e_mcp_stdio`, `--test e2e_mcp_with_kg`).

Single-file blast radius held (extended to: two TOMLs + one new test file + four new scripts, all in well-bounded subtrees). `forbidden_paths` blanket-bans `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` so the executor cannot accidentally touch parser or lifecycle code.

Pre-baked mutation checks (WO-0042 lesson `For planner` line 80) — `acceptance` includes two mutation-check entries naming the exact mutation (replace `[transport].command` with a non-existent path) and the expected failure mode (`PluginError::Spawn`). Verifier doesn't have to invent a mutation patch.
Cargo-test summary regex with alternation (WO-0038/0039/0042/0043 lesson) — every `acceptance_criteria` cargo-test entry uses `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. ... 0 failed|... tests run: ... passed'`.

No-mocks-of-critical-deps (root CLAUDE.md) — the new integration tests spawn REAL ast-grep + REAL probe MCP servers and exchange real JSON-RPC. No `tokio::process::Command` mocking. The breaker test technique (real-ENOENT for failure-branch coverage, WO-0043 lesson) is also applied in the mutation-check entries.

Reproducibility — both manifests pin a specific upstream tag/version + cite the upstream URL in a leading comment block, matching `plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml:1-14`. No `main`-branch refs. `acceptance_criteria` greps for `! grep -q '"main"'` to assert the constraint mechanically.
DEC-0005 module-coherence — the integration test file (~120 LOC) is one commit per file even if over the ~50 LOC soft target. Documented in scope_in commit ladder commit 4.

Test wall-time budget — the new tests do real subprocess work; if `npx -y` / `uvx` first-run fetches happen, they happen once per CI machine and are cached. The verify scripts log binary versions so the verifier can diagnose slow first-runs without needing a flake-quarantine.

Operator-actionable failure messages in verify scripts — both `scripts/verify/P2-W6-F0X.sh` print a clear install hint pointing at `scripts/devtools/install-<bin>.sh` if the binary is absent, rather than a cryptic `command not found`. Carries forward the `[FAIL] <feature>: <reason>` pattern.

Phase-log Phase-2 standing rules item 1 (stdout pristine on stdio MCP path) — N/A directly here since this WO doesn't touch the daemon's stdio path; the new integration tests use cargo-test's stdout for test results only and the verify scripts redirect to `/tmp/wo-0044-*.log` per the WO-0042/0043 logging convention.

Phase-log Phase-2 standing rules item 2 (no mocks of Serena/LSP/ast-grep/Probe/etc.) — explicitly enforced in scope_out + acceptance + lessons_applied; this is the FIRST WO to live-test ast-grep + probe integration, so the no-mocks invariant is load-bearing here.
